data.title: Lindy AI: Why This Assistant Actually Runs Your Workday
data.excerpt: Lindy AI automates your inbox, meetings, and workflows 24/7. Here's why teams and solopreneurs are switching — and what makes it genuinely different.

$media.alt: Lindy AI: Why This Assistant Actually Runs Your Workday

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you. I only recommend tools I genuinely find useful.*
Why I Started Using Lindy (And Why I Stayed)

I used to spend the first 90 minutes of every workday doing the same thing: triaging emails, prepping meeting notes, following up with leads in my CRM, and shuffling calendar invites. None of that work was creative. None of it moved the needle. But it had to get done.
Then I tried
Lindy
, and the math changed overnight.

Lindy is an AI assistant that sits inside your existing tools — your inbox, your calendar, your CRM — and handles the repetitive, time-sucking tasks that drain your focus. Not with clunky automations you have to configure for hours. It learns your patterns, understands context, and acts on your behalf. Drafting replies. Scheduling meetings. Prepping agendas. Following up with prospects.
If you've ever wished you could clone yourself just to handle the admin, Lindy is the closest thing to it. And at $49.99/month for the Plus plan, it's roughly 160x cheaper than a human assistant.
Try Lindy for free here
and see what 24/7 AI support actually feels like.
What Makes Lindy Different From Every Other AI Tool
Plenty of AI tools can write an email or summarize a document. Lindy goes further because it operates as an
autonomous agent
, not just a chatbot. Here's what that means in practice:
It connects to your real stack.

Lindy plugs into 100+ integrations — Gmail, Outlook, Slack, Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Calendar, Zoom, and more. It doesn't ask you to copy-paste context into a prompt. It already knows what's in your inbox, who you're meeting with, and what stage your deals are at.
It acts without hand-holding.

You don't need to prompt Lindy for every single action. You set preferences once — tone of voice, response rules, scheduling windows — and it handles the rest. Emails get drafted. Meetings get booked. Follow-ups go out on time.
It works across multiple inboxes.
The Plus plan supports up to two inboxes, so you can manage personal and work accounts without switching tools. The Pro plan expands that even further.
It communicates where you already are.
You can interact with Lindy via chat, iMessage, or SMS. No need to open another app. Just text your assistant the way you'd text a human one.
The result? You stop being a project manager of your own to-do list and start focusing on the work that actually matters. If you want a deeper walkthrough, check out our
step-by-step guide on how to use Lindy
Core Features That Save You Hours Every Week
Let's get specific. Here are the Lindy features I use daily and the exact time they reclaim:
Email Drafting

Lindy reads your incoming emails, understands the context, and drafts replies in your voice. You review, tweak if needed, and send. I went from spending 45 minutes on morning email triage to about 8 minutes. That's roughly 3 hours saved per week — on email alone.
Meeting Scheduling

Instead of the back-and-forth "does Tuesday work?" dance, Lindy handles it. It checks your availability, proposes slots to the other party, and confirms the meeting. It even respects your scheduling preferences (no meetings before 10am, buffer time between calls, etc.).
Meeting Note-Taking

Lindy joins your meetings, captures notes, extracts action items, and sends a summary afterward. You stay present in the conversation instead of frantically typing. This alone transformed how my team handles accountability — everyone gets the same recap, with clear ownership of next steps.
Meeting Prep and Follow-Up

Before a meeting, Lindy pulls relevant context: recent emails with the attendee, deal status in your CRM, notes from previous calls. After the meeting, it drafts follow-up emails referencing what was discussed. No more "I'll send notes later" promises that slip through the cracks.
CRM Updates

This one's huge for anyone in sales or partnerships. Lindy updates contact records, logs activities, and keeps your pipeline current — without you touching the CRM. It reads your conversations and reflects them in the system automatically.
If you're curious about creative ways to combine Lindy with other tools, our
Lindy use cases guide
has some real examples that go beyond the basics.
New and Standout Features You'll Love
Lindy ships updates fast. The team clearly listens to user feedback, and the product evolves in ways that matter. Here are some standout additions:
Autonomous agents for custom workflows.

Beyond email and meetings, you can now build Lindy agents that handle specific workflows — like onboarding new clients, processing inbound leads, or managing content approvals. You define the trigger and the steps; Lindy executes.
SMS and iMessage integration.
Most AI assistants live in a browser tab. Lindy lives in your pocket. The ability to text your AI assistant and get instant responses changes how you interact with your tools throughout the day.
Multi-inbox management.
Juggling a personal email, a work email, and maybe a side project inbox? Lindy handles them all, each with its own context and rules.
Smart calendar intelligence.
It doesn't just find open slots — it considers meeting prep time, travel time, focus blocks, and your energy patterns. If you protect your mornings for deep work, Lindy knows not to book calls there.
For the latest feature drops, bookmark our
Lindy new features page
. The team ships frequently, and some of the recent additions have been genuinely impressive.
Ready to hand off your busywork? Sign up for Lindy and start reclaiming your time today
Who Is Lindy Ideal For?
Lindy isn't trying to be everything to everyone. It's laser-focused on people whose days get eaten by communication and coordination. Here's who benefits most:
Solopreneurs and freelancers.

You're running every part of the business yourself. Lindy acts as a virtual assistant that doesn't need training, doesn't take sick days, and costs a fraction of a human hire. If your revenue depends on staying responsive to clients while also doing the actual work, Lindy bridges that gap.
Small teams without a dedicated EA.
You have 5-20 people, no executive assistant budget, and everyone is drowning in coordination. Lindy handles scheduling, follow-ups, and email drafting so your team can focus on execution.
Sales professionals and account managers.
The CRM updates alone are worth the subscription. But combined with meeting prep, follow-up drafting, and inbox management, Lindy becomes an unfair advantage in pipeline management.
Busy founders and executives.
Your inbox is a firehose. Lindy triages, drafts, and flags what actually needs your attention. It's the difference between being reactive and being strategic with your time.
Anyone experimenting with AI automation.

If you've been curious about AI agents but find most tools too technical or abstract, Lindy is a practical on-ramp. It doesn't require coding, prompt engineering, or complex setup. You connect your tools, set preferences, and it starts working.
Need tips on getting the most out of it? Our
Lindy tips and best practices guide
covers everything from setup to advanced workflows.
How Lindy Compares to a Human Assistant

Let's address this directly, because the pricing page makes a clever comparison. A human assistant costs roughly $8,000/month. They take months to train. They reply in hours. They work 9-5. They take sick days. They handle one task at a time.
Lindy at $49.99/month (Plus plan) or with the Pro plan for heavier usage works 24/7, replies in seconds, handles multiple inboxes, and learns your preferences in days, not months.

Now — is Lindy a replacement for a senior EA managing complex board communications and personal errands? Probably not. But for the 80% of assistant work that's repetitive and process-driven? It handles it brilliantly. And it never forgets a follow-up.
If you want to understand the full picture of what Lindy is and how it works under the hood, check out our
What is Lindy explainer
Getting Started Takes Less Than 5 Minutes
Here's what the setup actually looks like:
Sign up
at
lindy.ai
— the free trial lets you test the core features immediately.
Connect your tools
— Gmail, calendar, CRM, Slack. The integration flow is straightforward and takes about 2 minutes.
Set your preferences
— tone of voice, scheduling rules, which emails to prioritize.
Let Lindy work.
It starts observing your patterns and taking action within the first day.

Most people I've talked to say they felt the impact within 48 hours. Not because Lindy does anything magical — but because it quietly handles the dozens of small tasks you've been doing manually for years.
Lindy's free trial is live — grab it here before you forget
. The time you'll save this week alone justifies the five minutes of setup.

children.text: How much does Lindy AI cost?

Lindy offers a Plus plan at $49.99/month that includes up to 2 inboxes, email drafting, meeting scheduling, note-taking, and 100+ integrations. There's also a Pro plan for heavier usage. A free trial is available so you can test it before committing.
Can Lindy AI replace a human virtual assistant?

For repetitive, process-driven tasks like email triage, meeting scheduling, CRM updates, and follow-ups, Lindy handles the work extremely well — and at a fraction of the cost. For complex, nuanced executive support, a human EA still has the edge. Most teams find Lindy covers 80% of what they needed an assistant for.
What tools does Lindy integrate with?

Lindy connects with 100+ tools including Gmail, Outlook, Google Calendar, Slack, Zoom, Salesforce, HubSpot, and many more. You can also interact with Lindy via iMessage and SMS, so it works wherever you already communicate.
How long does it take to set up Lindy?

Most users are up and running in under 5 minutes. You connect your email, calendar, and CRM, set your preferences for tone and scheduling rules, and Lindy starts observing your patterns and taking action within the first day.
